headline: MACC independence undermined by PM appointment power
context: Reform groups highlighted that the Prime Minister's power to appoint the MACC chief commissioner undermines the anti-corruption body's independence.

cards.big: PM appoints MACC chief commissioner with no parliamentary confirmation required
cards.sub: Anti-corruption watchdog's independence compromised by executive appointment

cards.big: MACC Act 2009 Section 5 gives PM sole authority over chief commissioner appointment
cards.sub: No requirement for bipartisan consultation or parliamentary oversight

cards.big: Jakarta Statement on anti-corruption agency independence requires security of tenure
cards.sub: MACC commissioners serve at pleasure of appointing authority

cards.big: MACC prosecution success rate dropped to 62% in 2024 from 78% in 2020
cards.sub: Critics argue political considerations influence case selection and prosecution

cards.big: An anti-corruption body appointed by those it investigates cannot be independent
Parliamentary Select Committee on Governance recommended reform in December 2024
